reason: Even granting that B(H)_sa is a complete normed (Banach) space, the node's inference 'It is moreover a Banach algebra (B(H)_sa is a complete order-unit space)' is a non-sequitur as stated: completeness of the BASE space B(H)_sa does not by itself make End(B(H)_sa) a Banach algebra. The missing intermediate fact is that the space of bounded linear operators on a Banach space, equipped with the induced operator norm sup{||Ta||:||a||<=1}, is ITSELF complete. This is a genuine functional-analysis theorem (completeness of the codomain B(H)_sa is used to show Cauchy sequences of operators converge pointwise to a bounded operator), and per the project rule 'no standard facts' it must be provenanced or derived. GT-order-unit-norm (HOS 368-372) concerns the order norm on an order-unit space; it says NOTHING about endomorphism spaces, operator norms, or their completeness, so it cannot supply this step. Notably the required fact IS available in ground truth at Kitaev refs/kitaev-2405.02434/approximate_algebras.tex:638-642, which states 'Since calA is a Banach space, linear operators on it have a standard norm ||w||=sup_{||Z||<=1}||w(Z)||, and bounded linear operators form a Banach algebra' -- exactly the End(Banach space)=Banach algebra fact. But node 1.1.2 does NOT cite Kitaev:638-642 for this; it attributes the whole Banach-algebra claim to GT-order-unit-norm. The node should either cite Kitaev:638-642 (instantiated at calA=B(H)_sa) or otherwise provenance the End-is-complete step; as written there is an unjustified logical gap between 'B(H)_sa complete' and 'End(B(H)_sa) is a Banach algebra'. This is load-bearing: nodes 1.2 (series convergence of R), 1.3 (norm-limit of polynomials, R^2=(S^2)^(-1)) and 1.6 all rely on End(B(H)_sa) being COMPLETE, and they cite node 1.1.2 as the source of that completeness.
